scenario: dolt work session - audit what retains access into the core database object before a lifecycle rework
score_rationale: Q1 and Q4 show Sense gave me findings I could not have reconstructed with grep in this session — interface-hidden retention like the GCRootsProvider map key, the RootSetter holders, and the two dbfactory globals, plus the hooksDatabase cycle from a single graph call — which is 9-10 territory; but Q2's eleven fallbacks are not incidental, since every holder I cited needed a Read for its field name, every satisfier needed a grep for 'var _', every wiring site needed a grep, and Q1's silent 53-vs-48 truncation meant I finished the exhaustive-audit task without ever confirming I had the whole list from the symbol I actually cared about.
q4_value: retained_via_interfaces on sense_blast. The entire ticket was 'find holders that keep the object behind a field typed as something else and never name the core type' — and that field handed me ~50 of them in two calls, including ones I am confident I would not have found by grep in this session: gcctx.GCSafepointController retaining a DoltDB as a MAP KEY via GCRootsProvider (gc_safepoint_controller.go:29), the three dtables RootSetter holders (conflicts_tables_prolly.go:620, conflicts_tables_root_objects.go:42, constraint_violations_prolly.go:127) whose files never import doltdb, sqlserver.metricsListener reaching DoltDB through ClusterStatusProvider (metrics_listener.go:45), and the two dbfactory process-global caches typed datas.Database (file.go:89, git_remote.go:99) that became my #1 critical finding. The carrier+chain strings from the datas.Database blast then let me write Task 5's mechanical traces without re-deriving each path. No grep query I can construct finds 'struct field typed as an interface that some type containing DoltDB satisfies'.
q5_improve: Never silently trim retained_via_interfaces — and if you must, say which rows were dropped and how to get them. The DoltDB blast reported count:53 with retained_trimmed:true and shipped 48 rows; re-running with max_hops=1 returned 40, a subset, so the tool actively resisted completion. On a task whose stated stakes are 'a holder you saw but did not write down is a holder missed', an unpaginated truncated list is a correctness bug, not a formatting one. Give me a cursor, or a strict-superset mode, or at minimum name the omitted symbols. Second, closely related: populate carrier+chain on EVERY blast (the datas.Database call had them, the doltdb.DoltDB call did not, for the same underlying graph) and add the field name/type at the holder's ref line — that one addition would have eliminated the five batch struct-body reads that were the bulk of my file I/O this session.

score_rationale: Per Q1/Q4, Sense surfaced the interface-typed holders that are unfindable by grep in principle — no literal to search for — which is 9-10 territory, but per Q1/Q2 every one of the ~130 refs needed a grep pass to convert struct lines into the field lines the task demanded, it silently dropped GitServerWatcherConfig from an otherwise complete family, and ~40% of rows were audit-log noise I filtered by hand, so it lands at the top of the 'faster, but I still needed fallbacks to finish' band rather than above it.
q4_value: retained_via_interfaces is the whole reason this audit was tractable. The ticket's hard case — a holder whose field is a locally declared interface, in a file that never imports lib/backend or lib/cache and never names their types — is invisible to grep by construction: there is no string to search for. Sense gave me ~130 such holders across 9 areas in four paged calls, including families I would not have thought to look for (the 11 authclient per-role wrappers, the 37 auth v1 services each with its own bespoke Cache/Reader/Backend interface). Grep could only have found these by me first guessing every interface name, which is circular.
q5_improve: Anchor retained_via_interfaces refs to the retaining FIELD, not the struct declaration — and emit the field name and its declared type alongside. The ticket asked for 'the file:line of its retaining field', and the tool gave me the struct line, so I spent the majority of the session running bulk greps to convert ~70 struct refs into field refs (server.go:58→:66, forwarder.go:116→:128, sessions.go:703→:707). The tool already knows which field created the edge; surfacing it would have made the output directly citable and cut this session roughly in half. Secondary: make include_tests:false actually filter retained_via_interfaces, and rank or flag the auth.Server-carrier rows whose via is an audit/streaming interface so the storage-relevant signal isn't diluted ~40%.

q1_accurate.note: retained_via_interfaces located the entire narrowed-handle family I cited: the 11 authclient wrappers (lib/auth/authclient/api.go:1589-1917), the auth v1 service configs (crownjewelv1 Reader, notificationsv1 Backend, machineidv1 BotInstancesCache, workloadidentityv1 issuerCache, etc.), the lib/services/watcher.go getter-embedding configs, and the agent-side AccessPoint holders (lib/srv/app, lib/srv/db, lib/srv/desktop, lib/kube/proxy). None of these files name backend.Backend.

Missed lib/services/watcher.go:1930 GitServerWatcherConfig / GitServerGetter, which is structurally identical to the 15 sibling watcher configs it did return. I found it only by grepping the embedded-getter pattern across the file.

Large false-positive tier: ~40% of the 375 rows are audit-log/streaming holders (events.SessionWriter via Stream, events.MultiLog via AuditLogger, filesessions.UploaderConfig via Streamer, eventstest.MockUploader via MultipartUploader) that 'retain' storage only because carrier auth.Server composes it. I had to manually filter these out of the audit.

include_tests:false did not exclude test files from retained_via_interfaces — page 3 (offset 241) was over half *_test.go rows (accesslist.mockClient, auth_test.mockCache, proxy.mockAccessPoint...), and the offset-363 tail was 12/12 test rows. The flag appears to gate affected_tests only.

q2_fallbacks.missing: Field-level line numbers. Refs anchored to the struct decl, so I ran three bulk greps (~70 file+type pairs) to recover the actual retaining field lines — this was the single largest chunk of the session.
Completeness within an obvious family. Grepping '^\t[A-Za-z]+Getter$' over lib/services/watcher.go surfaced GitServerGetter at :1930, absent from the retained set.

Whether a 'composes Cache' row means a concrete pointer field or something else. I sed'd lib/scopes/cache/assignments/assignment_cache.go:49-54 and roles/role_cache.go:38-42 to confirm they hold *cache.Cache[...] generics from lib/scopes/cache, a DIFFERENT Cache than lib/cache.Cache — the blast did not disambiguate which of the two same-named types it had traversed.

Confirmation that InventoryCacheConfig holds storage twice. Reading lib/cache/inventory/inventory_cache.go:325-350 showed both PrimaryCache *cache.Cache (:327) and BotInstanceBackend services.BotInstance (:339); the retained row listed only the BotInstance path.

Field type for holders where my type-anchored grep returned nothing — sed/grep on discoveryconfigv1, healthcheckconfigv1, vnetconfigv1, x509_overrides_service, appauthconfigv1, scopes/access, scopes/joining showed the fields are named 'Backend'/'Storage'/'Reader' with a services.* qualified type, which my first regex pass missed.
